checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison between the source PDF and the converted JSON output confirms that every sentence and paragraph in the output is derived directly from the source. No fabricated content or sections have been added by the pipeline.
The source document contains no contact information such as phone numbers or email addresses, and the JSON output correctly reflects this absence without inventing any data.

The output contains all sections from the four-page source document, including the attendance record and all 14 meeting items. Voting records for specific items (4, 5, and 6) are also fully preserved.
The tables in the JSON match the source tables row-for-row. The main attendance table contains all 27 names (10 members, 17 staff), and the voting tables correctly list all 10 participating members.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All critical text, such as the full descriptions of the committee's goals in item 6 and the project updates in items 7 through 12, has been captured without omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output is a faithful transcription. Specific details like dates (e.g., in item 5), financial figures (item 9), and personal names are accurate to the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The content of the document is not duplicated. While the vote tables for items 4, 5, and 6 contain identical member names and votes, these correspond to distinct tables appearing on different pages/sections of the source PDF. No paragraphs or sections are erroneously repeated in the output.

The heading hierarchy is incorrect. The main document title and subsequent headers like 'Minutes Vote' are assigned level 3 (H3). There are no level 1 or level 2 headings present in the element structure, which skips levels and fails to provide a proper structural outline for screen readers.
checks.b_evidence_source: North Carolina 911 Board Technology Committee Meeting MINUTES...

checks.a_analysis: The table structures for the attendance list and the voting records are correctly preserved. The complex attendance table properly identifies headers and uses row spans for group labels ('Members', 'Staff'). The vote tables correctly capture the two-column member/vote relationship. The addition of column headers like 'Name' or 'Vote' for accessibility is an acceptable change.

The reading order of the output follows the logical progression of the source document, moving from the header and attendance section to the individual meeting items and their respective vote tables in the correct sequence.

The conversion of the numbered list (items 1-14) is structurally broken. Items 1-4 are in an ordered list, items 5 and 6 are rendered as separate paragraphs, and items 7-14 are in a second ordered list. This causes the list to lose its numbering continuity for assistive technologies. Additionally, the 'Goals' listed under item 6 are converted to individual paragraphs rather than being maintained as a sub-list.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Chair’s Welcome... 5. 2022 Technology Committee Dates Vote... 7. ESInet Update... Goal 1A:...
checks.b_evidence_output: The items are split between list elements (indices 0 and 16) and paragraph elements (indices 6 and 9) in the 'Meeting Items' section. Goals are at indices 10-14.

checks.a_analysis: There are no links in the source document, and no links have been injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: In the source PDF, the meeting items follow a continuous sequential numbering from 1 to 14. In the HTML output, this organization is significantly disrupted. Items 1-4 are grouped into an initial ordered list. Items 5 and 6 are rendered as individual headings. Finally, items 7 through 14 are grouped into a second ordered list that resets its numbering to 1 (rendering items 7-14 as 1-8). This visual rearrangement breaks the logical flow of the document and makes referencing items by number (a key feature of meeting minutes) difficult.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a sequential list of meeting items from '1. Chair's Welcome...' (page 2) to '14. Adjourn' (page 4).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows a list of items 1-4 under 'Meeting Items', followed by separate sections for items 5 and 6, and then a new ordered list starting at 1 for the remaining items ('1. ESInet Update' through '8. Adjourn').
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the 'Meeting Items' section and following lists in the HTML document.

checks.a_analysis: All tables, including the Attendance table and the various Vote tables, are rendered correctly. Columns are aligned, headers are distinct, and the data (such as 'P', 'NP', 'Y') is accurately placed within the cells. The tables remain readable and understandable.

The NC DIT 911 Board logo is present and correctly rendered in the HTML. The red 'Approved 2/10/2022' watermarks seen in the PDF are absent, which is an expected accessibility improvement for decorative/background elements and is not considered a defect.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. While the document title appears at the very top and again in the header area, this is consistent with accessible design for navigation landmarks and document identification, and does not constitute a defect of body content duplication.
